lessons.title: Decision Tree Analysis: build the decision from the evidence
lessons.dek: A source-disciplined build that connects inputs, dependencies, controls, and a scenario to one decision output.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: A reported or observed value whose document, field, period, and unit are identified before it enters the model.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Treating a forecast or management target as if it were a historical fact.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: A forward-looking judgment entered deliberately into the model and exposed for review or scenario change.
definitionCatalog.whyItMatters: It makes uncertainty visible and testable.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Hiding an assumption inside a formula or hardcoding it into an output.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: The single defined line item, schedule, or input register from which downstream model references should flow.
definitionCatalog.whyItMatters: It prevents conflicting versions of the same economic quantity.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Re-entering the same value in several schedules.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: A causal or computational relationship in which one model output supplies an input to another calculation.
definitionCatalog.whyItMatters: It explains how a scenario reaches the decision output.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Showing formulas without identifying the order of transmission.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: A test of an accounting identity, roll-forward, sign convention, or scenario relationship that should hold if the build is linked correctly.
definitionCatalog.whyItMatters: It distinguishes a functioning model from a formatted worksheet.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Using a balance check as proof that the assumptions are economically valid.

definitionCatalog.formalDefinition: The traced movement of one changed assumption through every material downstream dependency to the final decision output.
definitionCatalog.whyItMatters: It converts a model from a static answer into an instrument for reasoning.
definitionCatalog.commonExaminationError: Changing several assumptions at once and attributing the result to one driver.

decision_tree_analysis-scenario.text: Scenario change for Decision Tree Analysis: change one explicit assumption and trace the result through the dependency architecture.

lessons.objective: Build Decision Tree Analysis as a decision instrument for MBA finance work. The model must show which source inputs drive the output, how the dependencies transmit a change, which checks establish internal integrity, and which assumptions can reverse the decision.

inputs.name: FY2024-25 consolidated revenue
inputs.sourceField: Chairman and Managing Director statement, Strong and Resilient Performance

inputs.name: FY2024-25 consolidated EBITDA
inputs.sourceField: Chairman and Managing Director statement, Strong and Resilient Performance

inputs.name: FY2024-25 consolidated net profit
inputs.sourceField: Chairman and Managing Director statement, Strong and Resilient Performance

inputs.name: Gross debt at March 31, 2025
inputs.sourceField: Financial Performance and Review, balance-sheet highlights

buildOrder.title: Define the decision and model boundary
buildOrder.detail: State the output, horizon, currency, unit, and exclusions for Decision Tree Analysis before selecting formulas.

buildOrder.title: Load and classify source inputs
buildOrder.detail: Enter historical facts with document fields and dates, distinguish observable market inputs from assumptions, and establish one source of truth for each quantity.

buildOrder.title: Build the operating or valuation mechanics
buildOrder.detail: Apply the mechanics in dependency order: Define decision and chance nodes, assign sourceable probabilities and cash flows, discount branch values consistently, roll expected values back, then sensitivity-test the critical assumptions.

buildOrder.title: Link the dependency architecture
buildOrder.detail: Connect each schedule or calculation to the prior output, expose feedback loops, and keep any circularity treatment explicit.

buildOrder.title: Run controls and transmit a scenario
buildOrder.detail: Run identity, roll-forward, unit, sign, and direction checks, then change one assumption and trace its effect to the decision output.

checks.title: Source and unit check
checks.detail: Every input has a source, as-of date or research task, classification, and unit that matches the calculation using it.

checks.title: Dependency check
checks.detail: Every material output references a named upstream input or schedule rather than a hardcoded result.

checks.title: Identity or roll-forward check
checks.detail: The relevant accounting or mathematical identity and each schedule roll-forward reconcile in every modeled period.

checks.title: Sign and direction check
checks.detail: A favorable and adverse change move each downstream line in the economically expected direction.

checks.title: Scenario consistency check
checks.detail: The scenario changes the stated assumption only and the final output movement can be explained through the dependency graph.

scenario.trace: Change one explicit assumption in Decision Tree Analysis, follow it through the named schedules and calculations, then record the exact decision output that changes and the reason for the change.

lessons.output: Use the completed Decision Tree Analysis build to compare the decision alternatives, identify the assumptions with the greatest effect, and state what evidence is still required before relying on the output.

auditQuestions.question: What decision does the Decision Tree Analysis build support?
auditQuestions.answer: How should mutually exclusive decisions be valued when each branch has a probability, timing, and cash-flow consequence? The model is useful only when the decision, horizon, currency, and output are stated before the first input is entered. A calculation without a named decision can produce a precise number that no committee can use.

auditQuestions.question: Which inputs are historical facts and which are assumptions?
auditQuestions.answer: Historical facts come from the cited filing, contract, regulatory record, or market observation and must retain their as-of date and unit. Assumptions are forward-looking judgments that must remain visible and changeable. Blending them makes an assumption look like evidence and prevents a reviewer from locating the model’s judgment.

auditQuestions.question: What is the first dependency that should be checked?
auditQuestions.answer: Start with the source of truth for the build. For Decision Tree Analysis, that means confirming the input definitions, scope, period, unit, and classification before constructing downstream outputs. A downstream formula cannot repair a wrong source field.

auditQuestions.question: How does the build avoid a hardcoded output?
auditQuestions.answer: Every material output must trace through the construction sequence described by the mechanics: Define decision and chance nodes, assign sourceable probabilities and cash flows, discount branch values consistently, roll expected values back, then sensitivity-test the critical assumptions. If an output changes without a named input or dependency changing, the model contains a hardcode, broken link, or hidden plug.

auditQuestions.question: Which integrity check would catch a broken dependency?
auditQuestions.answer: Use both an accounting or mathematical identity check and a direction-of-change check. The identity catches incomplete linkage. The direction check catches a model that balances while translating an economic change in the wrong direction.

auditQuestions.question: How should a scenario be transmitted through the model?
auditQuestions.answer: Change one explicitly named assumption, record the before and after value, then trace every material downstream line in dependency order. The final decision output must move for a stated reason. A before-and-after headline without the transmission path is not a model explanation.

auditQuestions.question: What would make the output misleading even if the checks pass?
auditQuestions.answer: A model can reconcile mechanically while using an inapplicable peer set, an inconsistent accounting definition, an unrealistic financing assumption, or an unexamined forecast relationship. Integrity checks prove internal consistency; they do not prove that the economic assumptions are true.

auditQuestions.question: What should a reviewer ask before relying on the result?
auditQuestions.answer: The reviewer should ask which input has the greatest effect, which source field is least reliable, what assumption breaks first under stress, and what evidence would reverse the conclusion. Those questions test whether the builder understands the model rather than merely reproducing its formulas.

sections.heading: Decision Objective
body: Build Decision Tree Analysis to answer a bounded MBA finance decision: How should mutually exclusive decisions be valued when each branch has a probability, timing, and cash-flow consequence?. The output must be decision-relevant, source-traceable, and sensitive to the assumptions that actually drive it.

sections.heading: Source Inputs
body: The input register separates historical facts, observable market inputs, and explicit assumptions. Any live field not present in the cited source remains a visible research task rather than an invented number.

sections.heading: Construction Sequence
body: The build follows this mechanics stub: Define decision and chance nodes, assign sourceable probabilities and cash flows, discount branch values consistently, roll expected values back, then sensitivity-test the critical assumptions.
Each step produces a named output that becomes an input to the next dependent step.

sections.heading: Dependency Architecture
body: The architecture identifies the source of truth, the forward links, and any feedback loop that can create circularity.

sections.heading: Model Integrity Checks
body: A reviewer tests accounting identities, roll-forwards, source links, units, signs, and scenario direction before interpreting the final output.

sections.heading: Scenario Transmission
body: Change one material assumption, trace its effect through the dependency graph, and identify the output that changes the decision.

sections.heading: Decision Interpretation
body: The output answers the stated question only within the model boundary. It does not establish facts that were not sourced, nor does it remove the need to challenge the assumptions behind Decision Tree Analysis.

sections.heading: Model Review Questions
body: The review drill tests whether the builder understands source discipline, dependency order, integrity checks, scenario movement, and the model’s limits.

generationAudit.sourceInputStatus: real historical inputs where available; uncaptured live fields exposed as research tasks

buildOrder.title: Define the decision and model boundary
buildOrder.detail: State the output, horizon, currency, unit, and exclusions for Internal Rate of Return before selecting formulas.

buildOrder.title: Load and classify source inputs
buildOrder.detail: Enter historical facts with document fields and dates, distinguish observable market inputs from assumptions, and establish one source of truth for each quantity.

buildOrder.title: Build the operating or valuation mechanics
buildOrder.detail: Apply the mechanics in dependency order: Enter dated cash flows, solve for the rate that sets NPV to zero, identify multiple-IRR or reinvestment problems, and compare the decision with NPV at the appropriate cost of capital.

buildOrder.title: Link the dependency architecture
buildOrder.detail: Connect each schedule or calculation to the prior output, expose feedback loops, and keep any circularity treatment explicit.

buildOrder.title: Run controls and transmit a scenario
buildOrder.detail: Run identity, roll-forward, unit, sign, and direction checks, then change one assumption and trace its effect to the decision output.

checks.title: Identity or roll-forward check
checks.detail: The relevant accounting or mathematical identity and each schedule roll-forward reconcile in every modeled period.

checks.title: Sign and direction check
checks.detail: A favorable and adverse change move each downstream line in the economically expected direction.

checks.title: Scenario consistency check
checks.detail: The scenario changes the stated assumption only and the final output movement can be explained through the dependency graph.

scenario.trace: Change one explicit assumption in Internal Rate of Return, follow it through the named schedules and calculations, then record the exact decision output that changes and the reason for the change.

lessons.output: Use the completed Internal Rate of Return build to compare the decision alternatives, identify the assumptions with the greatest effect, and state what evidence is still required before relying on the output.

auditQuestions.question: What decision does the Internal Rate of Return build support?
auditQuestions.answer: What discount rate makes an investment’s dated cash flows equal to zero in present-value terms, and when does that rate mis-rank projects? The model is useful only when the decision, horizon, currency, and output are stated before the first input is entered. A calculation without a named decision can produce a precise number that no committee can use.

auditQuestions.question: Which inputs are historical facts and which are assumptions?
auditQuestions.answer: Historical facts come from the cited filing, contract, regulatory record, or market observation and must retain their as-of date and unit. Assumptions are forward-looking judgments that must remain visible and changeable. Blending them makes an assumption look like evidence and prevents a reviewer from locating the model’s judgment.

auditQuestions.question: What is the first dependency that should be checked?
auditQuestions.answer: Start with the source of truth for the build. For Internal Rate of Return, that means confirming the input definitions, scope, period, unit, and classification before constructing downstream outputs. A downstream formula cannot repair a wrong source field.

auditQuestions.question: How does the build avoid a hardcoded output?
auditQuestions.answer: Every material output must trace through the construction sequence described by the mechanics: Enter dated cash flows, solve for the rate that sets NPV to zero, identify multiple-IRR or reinvestment problems, and compare the decision with NPV at the appropriate cost of capital. If an output changes without a named input or dependency changing, the model contains a hardcode, broken link, or hidden plug.

auditQuestions.question: Which integrity check would catch a broken dependency?
auditQuestions.answer: Use both an accounting or mathematical identity check and a direction-of-change check. The identity catches incomplete linkage. The direction check catches a model that balances while translating an economic change in the wrong direction.

auditQuestions.question: How should a scenario be transmitted through the model?
auditQuestions.answer: Change one explicitly named assumption, record the before and after value, then trace every material downstream line in dependency order. The final decision output must move for a stated reason. A before-and-after headline without the transmission path is not a model explanation.

auditQuestions.question: What would make the output misleading even if the checks pass?
auditQuestions.answer: A model can reconcile mechanically while using an inapplicable peer set, an inconsistent accounting definition, an unrealistic financing assumption, or an unexamined forecast relationship. Integrity checks prove internal consistency; they do not prove that the economic assumptions are true.

auditQuestions.question: What should a reviewer ask before relying on the result?
auditQuestions.answer: The reviewer should ask which input has the greatest effect, which source field is least reliable, what assumption breaks first under stress, and what evidence would reverse the conclusion. Those questions test whether the builder understands the model rather than merely reproducing its formulas.

auditQuestions.question: What decision does the Risk-Adjusted NPV build support?
auditQuestions.answer: How does a decision change when risk is reflected explicitly in cash flows, discount rates, or scenario probabilities rather than ignored behind one headline forecast? The model is useful only when the decision, horizon, currency, and output are stated before the first input is entered. A calculation without a named decision can produce a precise number that no committee can use.

auditQuestions.question: Which inputs are historical facts and which are assumptions?

auditQuestions.answer: Start with the source of truth for the build. For Risk-Adjusted NPV, that means confirming the input definitions, scope, period, unit, and classification before constructing downstream outputs. A downstream formula cannot repair a wrong source field.

auditQuestions.question: How does the build avoid a hardcoded output?
auditQuestions.answer: Every material output must trace through the construction sequence described by the mechanics: Build base cash flows, identify the risk-adjustment method, apply probability, certainty-equivalent, or risk-adjusted-discount inputs consistently, then compare NPV across scenarios. If an output changes without a named input or dependency changing, the model contains a hardcode, broken link, or hidden plug.

auditQuestions.question: Which integrity check would catch a broken dependency?
auditQuestions.answer: Use both an accounting or mathematical identity check and a direction-of-change check. The identity catches incomplete linkage. The direction check catches a model that balances while translating an economic change in the wrong direction.

auditQuestions.question: How should a scenario be transmitted through the model?
auditQuestions.answer: Change one explicitly named assumption, record the before and after value, then trace every material downstream line in dependency order. The final decision output must move for a stated reason. A before-and-after headline without the transmission path is not a model explanation.

auditQuestions.question: What would make the output misleading even if the checks pass?
auditQuestions.answer: A model can reconcile mechanically while using an inapplicable peer set, an inconsistent accounting definition, an unrealistic financing assumption, or an unexamined forecast relationship. Integrity checks prove internal consistency; they do not prove that the economic assumptions are true.

auditQuestions.question: What should a reviewer ask before relying on the result?
auditQuestions.answer: The reviewer should ask which input has the greatest effect, which source field is least reliable, what assumption breaks first under stress, and what evidence would reverse the conclusion. Those questions test whether the builder understands the model rather than merely reproducing its formulas.
